summary:
The sphenoid bone closely resembles both externally and internally an image of something shaped akin to 'a bat with extended wings', due primarily to its distinctive shape featuring a main section surrounded by expansive projections similar to wings, along with intricate inner structures paralleling those found inside bats' bodies through extensive networks connecting various parts via channels comparable to nerve paths seen in actual bony frameworks mimicking biological analogues effectively here too.
